county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hancock County formed 1851 from Wright. County seat is Garner.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Garner holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Hancock County was settled in the 1870s-1880s, primarily by Scandinavian (Norwegian) and German immigrants. Pilot Knob State Park area. Britt was long the host of the National Hobo Convention. Hancock County Historical Society. Kanawha and Crystal Lake communities. East Fork of Des Moines River. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Hancock County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Garner
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Garner
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Garner / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Hancock County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Hancock County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Hancock County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Hancock County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Hancock County Auditor, Garner
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Hancock County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Hancock County Scandinavian Church Records -- Lutheran Congregations
source_profiles.repository: Luther College Archives, Decorah / ELCA Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian and Swedish Lutheran church records for Hancock County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials. Many Norwegian Synod and Hauge Synod congregations.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Hardin County formed 1851 from Black Hawk. County seat is Eldora.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Eldora holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Hardin County is centered on Eldora and Iowa Falls. Ellsworth College (now closed) in Iowa Falls had institutional records. State Training School in Eldora (est. 1868) -- juvenile institutional records at Iowa State Archives. Hardin County Historical Society. Ackley, Hubbard, and Steamboat Rock communities. Iowa River valley settlement. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Hardin County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / State Training School, Eldora (est. 1868)

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Harrison County formed 1851 from Pottawattamie. County seat is Logan.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Logan holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Harrison County along the Missouri River was part of the Mormon Trail corridor. Preparation Canyon State Park marks the site of the Preparation community, a splinter Latter-day Saint settlement (1853-1858). Missouri Valley and Dunlap communities. Harrison County Historical Society. Boyer River valley. Loess Hills ecology affected settlement patterns. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Harrison County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Logan / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Harrison County Historical Society

source_profiles.source_name: Harrison County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Harrison County Auditor, Logan
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Harrison County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Mormon Trail Records -- Harrison County Area
source_profiles.repository: LDS Church History Library, Salt Lake City / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Latter-day Saint emigrant records, pioneer journals, and camp records for the Harrison County area of the Mormon Trail across Iowa (1846-1869). Includes both LDS members and non-Mormon settlers who arrived along the same corridor.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Henry County formed 1836 from Des Moines. County seat is Mount Pleasant.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Mount Pleasant holds deeds from 1836; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Henry County is one of the earliest Iowa counties (1836). Mount Pleasant was an important educational center -- Iowa Wesleyan University (est. 1842) is one of the oldest in the state. Strong Quaker community at Salem -- Underground Railroad station with well-documented abolitionist activity. Henry County Heritage Trust. Swedish settlers in the New Sweden area. Skunk River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Henry County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1836. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Henry County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Henry County Quaker (Friends) Meeting Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Yearly Meeting / Swarthmore College Friends Historical Library / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Friends meeting records including minutes, membership lists, certificates of removal, birth/death/marriage registers. Henry County Quaker meetings were active in the Underground Railroad.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Howard County formed 1851 from Chickasaw, Floyd. County seat is Cresco.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Cresco holds deeds from 1855; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Howard County in northeastern Iowa was settled by Norwegians, Bohemians (Czechs), and Irish. Norwegian Lutheran church records are essential for many families. Czech Catholic church records in the Protivin area. Howard County Historical Society in Cresco. Lime Springs and Riceville communities. Upper Iowa River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Howard County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Humboldt County formed 1851 from Webster. County seat is Dakota City.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Dakota City holds deeds from 1857; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Humboldt County was settled in the 1860s-1870s, primarily by Scandinavian and German immigrants. Dakota City and Humboldt (the city) are twin communities. Humboldt County Historical Society. Rutland and Livermore communities. West Fork of the Des Moines River. Frank Gotch, world champion wrestler, was from Humboldt. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Humboldt County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Ida County formed 1851 from Cherokee. County seat is Ida Grove.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Ida Grove holds deeds from 1858; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Ida County was settled in the 1870s by German, Danish, and English immigrants. Ida Grove is known for its castles built by Byron Godbersen. Ida County Historical Society. Holstein (named for the German province) and Battle Creek communities. Maple River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Ida County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Iowa County formed 1843 from Johnson. County seat is Marengo.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Marengo holds deeds from 1845; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Iowa County was named for the Iowa (Ioway) people. Amana Colonies (est. 1855) -- the German Inspirationist communal society left extraordinary records: communal kitchen records, church registers, membership rolls, and property records at the Amana Heritage Society museum and archives. Amana records are essential for anyone with Inspirationist ancestry. Iowa County Historical Society in Marengo. Williamsburg and North English communities. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Iowa County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jackson County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Maquoketa.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Maquoketa holds deeds from 1838; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Jackson County was among the earliest organized in Iowa (1837). Maquoketa Caves area was early settlement. Andrew and Bellevue Mississippi River towns have early records. Jackson County Historical Society. German and Irish settlers predominate. Hurstville Lime Kilns historic site. Maquoketa River valley. St. Donatus, a Luxembourger settlement, has unique church records. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Jackson County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Jackson County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Jackson County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1840-1950 for Jackson County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Jackson County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Jackson County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Jackson County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Jackson County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Jackson County Auditor, Maquoketa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Jackson County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Jackson County Early Church Records -- Mississippi River Settlements
source_profiles.repository: Various -- diocesan archives, denominational archives, FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Church registers (baptisms, marriages, burials) from early Mississippi River settlements in Jackson County. Catholic, Lutheran, Methodist, and Presbyterian records. German and Irish congregations predominate.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jasper County formed 1846 from Mahaska. County seat is Newton.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Newton holds deeds from 1846; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Jasper County is centered on Newton, long the headquarters of Maytag Corporation (1893-2006). Maytag company records and employee histories are significant for family research. Fred Maytag Park. Jasper County Historical Society. Grinnell College (in Poweshiek County) proximity influences the area. Baxter, Colfax (mineral springs resort), and Monroe communities. Skunk River valley. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Jasper County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Deed Records -- County Recorder
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Newton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1846 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Newton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1846. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Newton / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1846. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Jasper County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1850-1950 for Jasper County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Jasper County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Jasper County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Jasper County Auditor, Newton
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Jasper County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Jasper County Mining Records and Mine Inspector Reports
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / State Historical Society of Iowa
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Mine inspector reports, coal company records, mine accident reports, and related court records for Jasper County coal mining communities. Iowa Mine Inspector annual reports list mines, operators, and sometimes workers.
